English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
American Demographics records 6794
2 cubic feet. 2 cubic feet.
1979-2004. Printed material Collection consists of records related to the publication of American Demographics. Includes reports, clippings, oral history recordings, and memorabilia. Collection material in English 8269704
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
October 2013
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, October 2013
Date modified:
Jude Corina, July 2017
Biographical / Historical
American Demographics was a journal founded in Ithaca, New York by Peter Francese in 1979. The magazine focused on analysis of demographic data and its interpretation for planners and marketers. The journal was published until 2004, when it was acquired by Advertising Age, which incorporated the name and some content into its magazine.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection consists of records related to the publication of American Demographics. Includes reports, clippings, oral history recordings, and memorabilia.
Cite As:
American Demographics records, #6794. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
United States -- Population -- Periodicals. Demographic surveys -- United States -- Periodicals. Demography -- Periodicals.
